findings.description: MockRiskManager approves ALL opportunities. No production guard prevents pipeline running with mock.
findings.recommendation: Add startup assertion refusing trade execution with MockRiskManager

findings.description: _execute_trade uses hardcoded $10k base_capital. Bypasses all RiskManager sizing. $300 account sizes 33x too large.
findings.recommendation: Wire calculate_position_size from RiskManager/CapitalAllocator

findings.description: _execute_trade has no second risk gate. If MockRiskManager was used, zero risk checks on actual execution.
findings.recommendation: Add mandatory non-mockable risk validation inside _execute_trade

findings.description: Emergency stop uses position.current_price which may be stale. In a crisis, closes at wrong price.
findings.recommendation: Fetch live prices before emergency close

findings.description: RiskManager never updates max_drawdown. Always returns 0.0. Drawdown never checked as trading gate.
findings.recommendation: Implement real-time drawdown tracking like TradingRiskManager

findings.description: Kelly cap 25% in RiskManager vs 10% in TradingRiskManager. Wrong class = 2.5x larger positions.
findings.recommendation: Unify Kelly cap. Recommend 5% for MICRO tier

findings.description: No per-trade maximum loss limit. Only portfolio-level. No guard saying never lose more than $X.
findings.recommendation: Add max_loss_per_trade parameter

findings.description: Partial fills treated as success (95% assumed). Remainder never cancelled, never tracked.
findings.recommendation: Implement fill-or-kill timeout

findings.description: If buy fills but sell fails, cancel may be impossible. Leaves unhedged directional exposure.
findings.recommendation: Attempt market sell to unwind. Log as critical risk event

findings.description: OrderExecutor accepts risk_manager but NEVER calls it. Field stored, never referenced.
findings.recommendation: Wire risk_manager into execute_arbitrage_trade

findings.description: DynamicRiskAdjustment not wired into pipeline. VaR thresholds and circuit breakers are dead code.
findings.recommendation: Import and integrate into LiveArbitragePipeline

findings.description: VaR and Expected Shortfall never consulted during trade decisions. Dead code in execution path.
findings.recommendation: Wire VaR limits into trade execution gate

findings.description: record_trade can drive current_capital below zero. No floor check. Negative allocations possible.
findings.recommendation: Add minimum capital floor ($50). Halt trading below floor

findings.description: No portfolio-level circuit breaker. Multiple strategies at 4.9% each = 15%+ portfolio drawdown with no trigger.
findings.recommendation: Add aggregate portfolio drawdown circuit breaker at 8%

findings.description: Quarterly rebalance resets ALL circuit breakers unconditionally. No recovery evidence required.
findings.recommendation: Only reset if strategy shows positive P&L in last 7 days

findings.description: Daily loss check uses abs() - triggers on large GAINS too. Logic bug.
findings.recommendation: Use < -max_daily_loss_pct, not abs()

findings.description: TradingRiskManager emergency_stop uses 0.1% slippage. In crash, slippage is 2-10%.
findings.recommendation: Use 3% crash slippage estimate

findings.description: Defaults to $10k capital ignoring config $300. min_diversification=5 impractical for $300.
findings.recommendation: Read from config, reduce to 2-3 for MICRO tier

findings.description: Paper trading uses own hardcoded risk constants. Not testing same risk path as live.
findings.recommendation: Use same risk management classes

findings.description: Paper sizing = MAX_POSITION_PCT * magnitude * confidence. Ignores Kelly entirely.
findings.recommendation: Use unified position sizing through risk layer

findings.description: Singleton get_risk_manager() has no locking. Race conditions possible.
findings.recommendation: Add threading.Lock around state mutations

findings.description: Two risk manager classes with different defaults, Kelly caps, drawdown logic, APIs.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate into single class

summary: 5 critical: MockRiskManager approves everything with no guard, $10k hardcoded bypassing all sizing, no second risk gate on execution, emergency stop uses stale prices, drawdown never tracked (always 0). VaR/ES/DynamicRisk are dead code in execution path. Risk management is largely theater for a $300 account.
recommendations: URGENT: Block MockRiskManager from trade execution path
URGENT: Replace hardcoded $10k with actual portfolio value
Wire VaR and DynamicRiskAdjustment into pipeline
Unify risk managers into single class
